forget_claims.C019: Chicken is safely cooked when its internal temperature reaches 165 degrees Fahrenheit.

retain_claims.C010: Heat denatures proteins and accelerates the chemical reactions that cook food.
retain_claims.C017: Bacteria multiply rapidly between 40 and 140 degrees Fahrenheit, a range called the danger zone.
retain_claims.C018: Cooking food to a high enough internal temperature kills harmful bacteria.
